17.1.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.1.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.1.title: First-Time Cloud and Emerging Technology Consultation

business.summary: A board-approved cloud strategy and governance framework ensures that cloud adoption decisions are aligned with the institution's risk appetite, technology strategy, and regulatory obligations.

requirements.requirement: Establish a board-approved cloud strategy that defines the institution's approach to cloud adoption, including strategic objectives, approved cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS), and alignment with overall technology strategy and risk appetite
requirements.rationale: A clear cloud strategy prevents ad-hoc cloud adoption that may introduce unassessed risks and ensures cloud investments support institutional strategic objectives

requirements.requirement: Define governance structures for cloud-related decisions including a cloud governance committee or equivalent body with authority over cloud adoption, architecture standards, and provider selection
requirements.rationale: Centralised governance prevents shadow IT cloud adoption and ensures consistent application of security, risk, and compliance requirements across all cloud deployments

requirements.requirement: Align the cloud strategy with the institution's risk appetite framework, defining acceptable risk levels for different categories of workloads and data classifications in cloud environments
requirements.rationale: Risk appetite alignment ensures cloud deployment decisions are consistent with the institution's overall risk tolerance and prevents critical workloads being placed in cloud environments beyond acceptable risk levels

technology.summary: Technology teams must implement the cloud strategy through approved architectures, standardised deployment patterns, and cloud management platforms that enforce governance controls.

requirements.requirement: Implement a cloud management platform that provides centralised visibility, governance enforcement, and policy compliance monitoring across all cloud environments
requirements.rationale: A centralised management platform enables consistent governance enforcement and prevents cloud deployments that violate institutional policies or regulatory requirements

requirements.requirement: Define and maintain approved cloud architecture patterns and reference architectures for common workload types, ensuring security and compliance requirements are embedded by design
requirements.rationale: Standardised architectures reduce the risk of misconfiguration and accelerate compliant cloud deployment by providing pre-approved patterns

requirements.requirement: Maintain an inventory of all approved cloud service providers and services, with documented assessments of each provider's security posture, compliance certifications, and data residency capabilities
requirements.rationale: An approved provider inventory prevents ad-hoc adoption of unevaluated cloud services and ensures all providers meet institutional and regulatory requirements

governance.summary: Governance frameworks must define clear decision rights, approval processes, and oversight mechanisms for cloud adoption to ensure regulatory compliance and strategic alignment.

requirements.requirement: Document a cloud governance framework defining decision rights, approval processes, roles and responsibilities, and oversight mechanisms for all cloud adoption and management activities
requirements.rationale: A documented governance framework ensures accountability and consistency in cloud decisions and provides auditable evidence of governance oversight

requirements.requirement: Require cloud adoption proposals to undergo a formal assessment covering risk, security, compliance, data residency, and cost before approval, with documented sign-off from relevant stakeholders
requirements.rationale: Formal assessment gates ensure every cloud adoption decision is informed by risk, security, and compliance considerations before commitment

requirements.requirement: Report cloud adoption status, risk metrics, and governance compliance to the board technology committee at least annually, including cloud spend, provider dependency, and security posture
requirements.rationale: Board reporting ensures governance visibility over the institution's growing cloud footprint and associated risk exposure

17.2.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.2.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.2.title: Subsequent Cloud and Emerging Technology Notification

business.summary: Implementing robust cloud-specific security controls aligned with the shared responsibility model protects the institution's data and systems in cloud environments against evolving cyber threats.

requirements.requirement: Implement security controls for all cloud environments covering identity and access management, data protection, network security, logging and monitoring, and configuration management
requirements.rationale: Cloud environments require specific security controls beyond traditional on-premises controls; gaps in any of these five domains can lead to data breaches or service compromises

requirements.requirement: Align all cloud security controls with the shared responsibility model, clearly delineating which controls are the institution's responsibility versus the cloud service provider's responsibility
requirements.rationale: Misunderstanding of shared responsibility boundaries is a leading cause of cloud security incidents; clear delineation ensures no security gaps between provider and institutional controls

requirements.requirement: Validate cloud security control effectiveness through regular assessments including penetration testing, configuration reviews, and compliance scanning at least annually
requirements.rationale: Regular validation ensures controls remain effective as cloud environments evolve and new services are adopted, preventing configuration drift and control degradation

technology.summary: Technical implementation must cover all five security domains with cloud-native and third-party tools, automated compliance monitoring, and continuous configuration validation.

requirements.requirement: Implement cloud-native and third-party security tools for identity and access management including multi-factor authentication, privileged access management, and least-privilege enforcement across all cloud accounts
requirements.rationale: IAM is the primary control plane in cloud environments; weak IAM controls are the most common vector for cloud security breaches

requirements.requirement: Deploy data protection controls including encryption at rest and in transit, data loss prevention, key management, and data classification enforcement in all cloud environments
requirements.rationale: Data protection controls ensure customer data confidentiality and integrity in cloud environments where the institution does not control the physical infrastructure

requirements.requirement: Implement cloud security posture management (CSPM) tools to continuously monitor cloud configurations against security baselines and detect misconfigurations
requirements.rationale: CSPM provides continuous visibility into cloud security posture and detects configuration drift before it can be exploited by threat actors

requirements.requirement: Configure comprehensive logging and monitoring across all cloud environments with centralised log aggregation, alerting, and integration with the institutional SIEM
requirements.rationale: Centralised logging and SIEM integration ensures security events in cloud environments are detected and responded to with the same rigour as on-premises events

governance.summary: Governance must define cloud security control standards, mandate regular assessments, and ensure shared responsibility mapping is documented and maintained for every cloud deployment.

requirements.requirement: Document a cloud security control framework defining minimum security requirements across all five domains (IAM, data protection, network security, logging, configuration management) for each cloud service model
requirements.rationale: A documented framework ensures consistent security control implementation across all cloud deployments and provides a baseline for assessment and audit

requirements.requirement: Maintain documented shared responsibility model mappings for each cloud service provider and service model, clearly assigning control ownership for every security domain
requirements.rationale: Documented mappings prevent assumptions about who is responsible for which controls and ensure no security gaps exist at the boundary

requirements.requirement: Schedule and conduct annual cloud security assessments including penetration testing of cloud environments, and report results to the board technology committee
requirements.rationale: Annual assessments validate that documented controls are implemented correctly and functioning as intended, providing assurance to governance bodies

17.3.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.3.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.3.title: Enhancement Notification Exemption

business.summary: Ensuring cloud portability and maintaining exit strategies for all cloud services protects the institution from excessive vendor dependency and guarantees continuity if a cloud provider relationship must be terminated.

requirements.requirement: Design cloud architectures that support portability and avoid excessive dependency on a single cloud service provider, using open standards and abstraction layers where practicable
requirements.rationale: Excessive vendor lock-in creates concentration risk and limits the institution's negotiating power and ability to respond to provider failures or regulatory concerns

requirements.requirement: Maintain documented exit strategies for all cloud services covering data extraction procedures, transition timelines, contingency arrangements, and estimated costs and resource requirements
requirements.rationale: Exit strategies are mandatory under RMiT; without them, the institution may be unable to migrate away from a cloud provider in a controlled manner if required

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic vendor lock-in risk assessments for each cloud service evaluating the degree of dependency, availability of alternatives, and feasibility of migration
requirements.rationale: Regular assessment ensures the institution maintains awareness of its dependency levels and can take proactive steps to reduce lock-in before it becomes critical

technology.summary: Technical architecture decisions must prioritise interoperability and portability, with validated data extraction capabilities and tested transition procedures for each cloud service.

requirements.requirement: Implement containerisation, infrastructure-as-code, and cloud-agnostic tooling where practicable to reduce dependency on provider-specific services and facilitate portability
requirements.rationale: Cloud-agnostic technologies reduce the technical effort and risk of migrating between providers, making exit strategies practically achievable

requirements.requirement: Validate data extraction procedures for each cloud service at least annually, ensuring customer data and institutional data can be fully extracted in standard formats within the exit strategy timeline
requirements.rationale: Untested data extraction procedures may fail during an actual exit, stranding data with the provider; annual validation ensures procedures remain current and functional

requirements.requirement: Maintain documented architecture diagrams showing provider-specific dependencies for each cloud deployment, identifying components that would require re-engineering during a provider transition
requirements.rationale: Dependency mapping enables accurate exit planning and cost estimation, and highlights areas where portability improvements should be prioritised

governance.summary: Governance must mandate exit strategy documentation for every cloud service, require periodic review and testing, and ensure vendor lock-in risks are reported to the board.

requirements.requirement: Require documented and board-acknowledged exit strategies as a prerequisite for approving any new cloud service adoption, with mandatory annual review of existing exit strategies
requirements.rationale: Mandating exit strategies at adoption prevents the common pattern of deploying to the cloud without considering how to leave, which becomes increasingly costly over time

requirements.requirement: Report vendor lock-in risk assessment results and exit strategy status to the board technology committee at least annually, including any identified portability concerns
requirements.rationale: Board reporting ensures governance visibility over concentration risk and vendor dependency, enabling informed strategic decisions about cloud provider relationships

requirements.requirement: Include cloud exit provisions in all cloud service contracts covering data return, data destruction, transition support, and minimum notice periods
requirements.rationale: Contractual exit provisions ensure the institution has legal rights to extract its data and receive provider support during a transition

17.4.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.4.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.4.title: Bank Discretionary Direction

business.summary: Establishing governance for AI and ML use in decision-making protects customers from biased or opaque automated decisions and manages the model risks inherent in deploying these technologies in financial services.

requirements.requirement: Establish a governance framework for the use of AI and ML in decision-making processes that addresses model risk management, data quality, bias detection, explainability, and accountability
requirements.rationale: AI and ML models in financial services can affect credit decisions, fraud detection, and customer outcomes; without governance, these models may produce biased, inaccurate, or unexplainable decisions

requirements.requirement: Subject all AI models used for customer-facing decisions to regular validation including accuracy testing, bias testing, and performance monitoring against defined thresholds
requirements.rationale: Customer-facing AI decisions have direct regulatory and reputational implications; regular validation ensures models continue to perform fairly and accurately as input data evolves

requirements.requirement: Ensure all AI-driven customer-facing decisions can be explained to affected customers and regulators in understandable terms, with documented explainability approaches for each model
requirements.rationale: Explainability is essential for regulatory compliance, customer trust, and the ability to challenge or appeal automated decisions

technology.summary: Technology platforms must support model lifecycle management, bias detection tooling, explainability capabilities, and continuous performance monitoring for all AI and ML models.

requirements.requirement: Implement a model lifecycle management platform that tracks AI and ML models from development through deployment, monitoring, and retirement with version control and audit trails
requirements.rationale: Model lifecycle management ensures traceability and accountability for all model decisions and enables rapid identification and rollback of underperforming models

requirements.requirement: Deploy bias detection and fairness testing tools that evaluate models against protected characteristics and defined fairness metrics before deployment and during ongoing operation
requirements.rationale: Automated bias detection identifies discriminatory patterns that may not be apparent through manual review, particularly across intersecting protected characteristics

requirements.requirement: Implement model performance monitoring dashboards that track accuracy, drift, fairness metrics, and anomalous outputs in real-time with automated alerting for threshold breaches
requirements.rationale: Continuous monitoring detects model degradation and emerging biases before they result in significant customer impact or regulatory exposure

governance.summary: Governance processes must define clear accountability for AI decisions, mandate regular model validation, and establish oversight mechanisms ensuring ethical and compliant use of AI in financial services.

requirements.requirement: Document an AI/ML governance framework defining model risk management requirements, data quality standards, bias testing requirements, explainability standards, and accountability structures
requirements.rationale: A documented framework provides consistent governance across all AI initiatives and ensures regulatory expectations for responsible AI are embedded in development practices

requirements.requirement: Establish a model risk committee or equivalent governance body with authority to approve, reject, or require modifications to AI models before deployment, particularly for customer-facing applications
requirements.rationale: Independent governance review prevents deployment of models that have not been adequately tested for bias, fairness, and accuracy

requirements.requirement: Maintain a comprehensive AI model inventory documenting all models in use, their purposes, data inputs, decision impact, validation status, and accountable owners
requirements.rationale: A centralised inventory provides governance visibility over all AI models and ensures none are deployed without appropriate oversight

17.5.section: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.5.subsection: Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology
17.5.title: Cloud and Emerging Technology Roadmap

business.summary: Conducting risk assessments before adopting emerging technologies ensures the institution understands the security, privacy, regulatory, and operational implications of new technologies before committing resources.

requirements.requirement: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments for the adoption of emerging technologies including distributed ledger technology, quantum computing preparedness, and Internet of Things deployments before committing to adoption
requirements.rationale: Emerging technologies carry novel and often poorly understood risks; pre-adoption assessment prevents the institution from discovering material risks after significant investment

requirements.requirement: Ensure emerging technology risk assessments cover security, privacy, regulatory compliance, operational, and strategic risk dimensions specific to the Malaysian financial services regulatory environment
requirements.rationale: Multi-dimensional risk assessment ensures no risk category is overlooked, particularly Malaysian-specific regulatory requirements that may restrict certain technology deployments

requirements.requirement: Maintain an emerging technology risk register that tracks all emerging technologies under evaluation or adopted, their risk assessments, and risk mitigation status
requirements.rationale: A centralised register provides governance visibility over the institution's emerging technology landscape and ensures risk assessments are completed before adoption proceeds

technology.summary: Technology teams must evaluate emerging technologies through structured proof-of-concept and risk assessment processes, with particular attention to cryptographic quantum readiness.

requirements.requirement: Establish a structured proof-of-concept and evaluation process for emerging technologies that includes security testing, performance assessment, and integration risk analysis in a controlled environment
requirements.rationale: Controlled evaluation prevents emerging technologies from being deployed into production without adequate testing and risk understanding

requirements.requirement: Conduct a quantum computing readiness assessment evaluating the institution's cryptographic dependencies, identifying quantum-vulnerable algorithms, and developing a migration roadmap to quantum-resistant cryptography
requirements.rationale: Quantum computing threatens current cryptographic standards; early assessment enables a planned transition to quantum-resistant algorithms before quantum threats materialise

requirements.requirement: Assess IoT security risks for any IoT deployments within the institution's environment, covering device authentication, firmware update mechanisms, network segmentation, and data privacy
requirements.rationale: IoT devices expand the attack surface significantly and often lack enterprise-grade security controls; specific risk assessment is essential before deployment

governance.summary: Governance frameworks must mandate risk assessments before emerging technology adoption, with clear approval gates and board reporting on the institution's emerging technology risk posture.

requirements.requirement: Document an emerging technology risk assessment framework defining assessment methodology, required risk dimensions, approval gates, and the process for evaluating and approving emerging technology adoption
requirements.rationale: A structured framework ensures consistent and comprehensive risk assessment across different emerging technologies and prevents ad-hoc adoption decisions

requirements.requirement: Require CRO and CISO sign-off on emerging technology risk assessments before adoption can proceed, ensuring risk and security implications are formally accepted
requirements.rationale: Formal risk acceptance by senior leaders ensures emerging technology risks are understood and accepted at an appropriate governance level

requirements.requirement: Report the emerging technology risk register and quantum computing readiness status to the board technology committee at least annually
requirements.rationale: Board reporting ensures governance awareness of emerging technology risks and the institution's preparedness for quantum computing impacts on cryptographic security
